MIRANDA, M.D., A MEDICAL CORPORATION'S, MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM FROM COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. NOTICE OF NON-OPPOSITION FILED BY PLAINTIFF ON OCTOBER 28, 2015. ORDER SIGNED. MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2016 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Russell Robinson of Law Office of Russell A. Robinson telephone number 415-255-0462 for for Plaintiff Howard Blonsky.
Elisabeth Madden of Lynch, Gilardi & Grummer telephone number 415-273-2381 for all Defendants.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion or argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS EDWARD MIRANDA and EDWARD P. MIRANDA, M.D., A MEDICAL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication is denied in its entirety. Assuming without deciding that plaintiff Howard Blonsky has waived his untimeliness objection by responding to the motion on the merits, defendants' motion must still be denied because defendants failed to meet their burden of production. Plaintiff's evidentiary objections to the declarations of Dr. Brian Parrett based on a lack of foundation and the declaration of Elisabeth Madden based on a lack of foundation and a failure to authenticate the attached medical records are sustained. (Garibay v. Hemmat (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 735.) As in Garibay, the medical records are not properly before the court. Dr. Parrett states that he relied on plaintiff's "medical records" and the "informed consent documents signed by Mr. Blonsky," yet he has not attached those records or otherwise identified them. Ms. Madden's declaration purports to attach the "informed consent documents and two "operative reports" prepared by Dr. Miranda. Ms. Madden's declaration suggests but does not state whether the records attached to that declaration are less than all of the records that Dr. Parrett relied upon. Regardless of whether Ms. Madden purports to attach all or only a portion of the medical records reviewed by Dr. Parrett, Ms. Madden has no foundation for authenticating the records attached to her declaration. Ms. Madden refers to Mr. Blonsky's responses to requests for admissions and suggests that Mr. Blonsky has admitted the genuineness of the informed consent documents. Mr. Blonsky's responses are vague and without review of the requests for admission which are not attached, they prove nothing.
Mr. Robinson is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
